items.label: LocalShout
items.content: Community platform (Next.js). Close to shipping but has been haphazard — scope kept expanding. Path to v1: drop remaining unfinished features behind feature flags, focus on App Store requirements (legal pages, compliance). A few targeted sessions should get it over the line. Has been distracted by tooling (OpenClaw slowed things down, stopped that). Hermes is the exciting accelerant once LocalShout ships.

items.label: SpoonsCount
items.content: pub check-in app (Angular/Firebase/Capacitor). On hold until LocalShout alpha ships.

items.label: Jimbo/Hermes
items.content: AI assistant. Daily briefing pipeline running well. Hermes framework is maturing and genuinely exciting — seen as the main accelerant for all projects once LocalShout ships. Currently: model of Marvin was stale, recalibrated April 2026. Velocity low (tasks not moving) — active work to make Jimbo actually useful day-to-day, not just ambient.

items.label: Hinge X
items.content: Dating parked until post-LocalShout launch. Wants to date from confidence (having shipped) rather than aspirationally. Recent potential date: no inclination to follow up.

items.label: NZ Passport
items.content: Application started but stalled. Two hours of admin work. Keeps getting forgotten — needs a nudge.

items.label: Explore leaving Watford
items.content: Feeling invisible and restless. Airbnb guest staying long-term opens subletting possibility. No concrete plan yet but the feeling is strong. Apps can be finished anywhere. Not a commitment — legitimising the thinking.

items.label: Note hygeine
items.content: Clean up your todos and your notes, we have a vault now, we should use it
Keep at it with Hinge - at the very least 10 mins

items.label: Fix LinkedIn positioning
items.content: Current profile says Frontend Developer / Angular specialist — massively undersells what I actually do. Update: (1) Headline → Product Engineer | AI, Full-Stack, React/Next.js, (2) About → rewrite to lead with end-to-end product building (LocalShout, Jimbo AI assistant, BuzzFeed), kill Angular/CSS pitch, (3) Add Independent Product Engineer role for LocalShout + Jimbo (most impressive work is invisible), (4) Update featured skills to Next.js, React, AI/LLM Integration, Full-Stack Dev, TypeScript, Node.js — kill Cypress and UX, (5) Update current role headline at BuzzFeed. This is a 2-hour task, not a week-long project. Do it, then get back to shipping.

items.label: Finances
items.content: Look at the finances properly — open the bank app, get aware of where things stand. Avoided this commitment last session; BuzzFeed gone now, no excuse.
Get finances in order — YNAB setup, track spending

BuzzFeed contract finished April 2026. Intentional gap — using it to focus on own projects. Comfortable with gaps as a freelancer but this one feels different: fewer contracts available, actively repositioning from frontend developer to AI solutions. Getting ideas out in public is the current push. Runway ~2 years, finances not urgent.
Open to good deals, error fares, creative routing. Specific window: Mat wants to go away 25 May–2 June 2026 — needs research now, about 4 weeks out. This is a priority: overdue for something fun.
Event planning — gigs, meetups, comedy well in advance so I can plan
Capture new ideas as they come

Actively repositioning from frontend developer to AI solutions. Not just a label change — genuinely building agents, pipelines, and products in this space. Getting ideas out in public is the current push. LinkedIn needs updating to reflect this.

items.label: Deferral pattern
items.content: Recurring tendency to defer social life, dating, friendships, health, and balance behind "once I ship LocalShout." This is a pattern, not a plan — the finish line keeps moving. Jimbo should gently challenge this when it comes up. The goal is a rich, balanced life with a partner and family eventually — that requires investment now, not later.

items.label: Gym
items.content: Been a few times. Target 2-3x/week. Pattern to watch: sometimes builds tools to track gym progress instead of just going. Half an hour is enough — keep it simple.

items.label: Pool
items.content: Sundays/Mondays/Tuesdays work best

items.label: Spanish
items.content: should be daily, even 10 mins

items.label: Walking
items.content: good weather? Suggest a walk (walkingclub.org.uk for routes)

items.label: Cooking
items.content: try recipes again: baba ganoush, other hits. Nudge when it's been a while.

items.label: Dating events
items.content: also try dateinadash.com, speed dating etc. alongside Hinge X

items.label: Decluttering
items.content: KonMari method in progress. Nudge to keep going.

items.label: Act!
items.content: Stop being the guy that thinks something nice to say /good to do, and then doesnt do it
Sentry bug alerts — irrelevant until LocalShout goes live.
Artemis repo notifications — useful reference but too noisy for regular consumption
Long-form reading (Taibbi, Steve Baker deep pieces) — want to but rarely have time

I want to launch real products that people use. LocalShout is the immediate focus — ship it. Spoons is paused but I think about it a lot and really want to get back to it. Product Hunt, indie dev communities, and developer tools are all relevant.
YNAB, budgeting, understanding where money goes. This is a current push — anything that helps with financial literacy or tools is welcome.

I love travelling and I'm good at finding deals. Error fares, creative routing, all-inclusive bargains, campervan relocations — I want to be the first to know about opportunities. Short-notice is fine.

Hobby project — a personal film collection tracker with availability checking. Want to grow it into something my cousin and I can both use. Next steps: add an HTTP API so Jimbo can push `to-watch` items from triage/email/chat directly into the collection. Longer term, extend the pattern to other "to-X" lists (reading, places, recipes). Lives at `~/development/film-planner/`.
Gigs, comedy, cinema, meetups, debates, hiking. I want a full calendar of interesting things to do. Small and niche beats big and commercial. Knowing early is everything — events sell out.
Tech moves fast. I want to stay current on frontend, AI/agents, and developer tools without drowning in newsletters. Quality over quantity. The occasional deep read when I have time.
Meetups, events, LinkedIn (passively). I'm not actively job hunting but I want to know what's out there and stay connected to the industry.
Background goal. Nutrition, fitness, mental health, stoicism. Not a daily priority but I appreciate nudges.
Weekly planning session looks at days 8-14, not 1-7. London slots fill on a 2-3 week horizon; planning at 1 week is too late and the resulting mediocrity erodes the habit.
Texture commitments (classes, gigs, social) are not a reward for completed work — they are the material a partner-ready life is built from. Work hard AND book the week.

active_tasks.title: Hook Anthropic SDK calls in Hermes to capture token usage
active_tasks.body: Intercept or wrap Anthropic SDK calls in Jimbo/Hermes to extract usage metadata and write to the token_usage table.
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- Directly serves the active Jimbo/Hermes project (top active project in snapshot) and sits under the explicit parent epic `note_6bfd014b` (Instrument Jimbo pipeline to capture token usage per dispatch), which is classified at priority 1.
- The task has fully-specified acceptance criteria: after each Anthropic SDK call, a token_usage record is written with dispatch_id, note_id, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens, and estimated_cost_usd — scope is locked and the done state is unambiguous.
- While the note carries manual_priority 2, all sibling tasks in this token-tracking epic (note_00aa9be8 DB schema, note_c01d15cd research, note_e63b7a32 SQL view) are classified at priority 1; this task is the core implementation step that makes the entire epic functional, so upgrading to priority 1 is consistent.
- No duplicates detected; this is a distinct subtask under the instrumentation epic, separate from the schema (note_00aa9be8) and reporting (note_d1e09925) layers.
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: After each Anthropic SDK call in the Hermes/dispatch pipeline, a token_usage record is written with correct dispatch_id, note_id, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens, and estimated_cost_usd.

active_tasks.title: When I change the name it doesn't apply everywhere
active_tasks.body: When I change the name it doesn't apply everywhere
active_tasks.raw_frontmatter: {"id":"note_8ea07fe7","tags":["development","bug"],"type":"task","title":"When I change the name it doesn't apply everywhere","scored":"2026-03-02T00:00:00.000Z","source":"google-tasks","status":"active","created":"2025-06-07T00:00:00.000Z","updated":"2025-06-07T00:00:00.000Z","priority":6,"processed":"2026-02-28T00:00:00.000Z","source_id":"YUZaMGZvMXRUeEZXNkNfVQ","source_list":"My Tasks","actionability":"vague","priority_reason":"A development bug, which aligns with the 'Build & Ship Products' goal. However, without knowing which specific active project it relates to, its urgency is moderate."}
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- LocalShout is the #1 active project targeting App Store/TestFlight; a data-consistency bug where name changes do not propagate across the app is a shipping blocker that warrants this-quarter priority.
- The note body is identical to the title with zero additional context — "the name" and "everywhere" are unspecified, making it impossible to define scope, reproduce, or confirm done state without further clarification.
- Existing record tags include `localshout` and `data-consistency`, suggesting this was previously inferred to belong to LocalShout; no parent epic covers rename/propagation bugs specifically.
- No duplicate notes found in active tasks, but the vagueness is severe enough that a developer could not act on this without Marvin clarifying which entity (user, venue, community, event) and which screens are affected.
active_tasks.suggested_ac: The name change should propagate correctly across all relevant parts of the application. No inconsistencies should be observed after a name update.

active_tasks.title: LocalShout: add Free / Covers filter to event listings
active_tasks.body: Add filter controls to the LocalShout event listing view so users can toggle between Free entry events and events with a cover charge. Filter should update results dynamically without a full page reload. Requires: (1) confirm events data model has a cover/free field or add one, (2) add filter UI (toggle or checkbox) accessible from the main listing, (3) wire filter to query/state so results update instantly.
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- LocalShout is the top active project with a TestFlight deadline end of April 2026, making this a this-quarter task by definition
- The task has a well-defined body with three concrete implementation steps (data model, UI, wiring) and acceptance criteria already written
- Filtering by Free/Covers is a user-facing feature that improves event discovery, directly relevant to shipping a polished v1
- manual_priority is already 1 and prior analysis agreed — no reason to diverge
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Event listings can be filtered by Free and Covers. Filter controls are visible from the main event view. Filtering updates results dynamically. Both filter states can be active simultaneously or toggled independently.

active_tasks.title: Build voice gym tracker PWA frontend (Web Speech API + history view)
active_tasks.body: Frontend piece. Static PWA hosted at fourfoldmedia.uk/apps/gym-tracker. Uses Web Speech API to listen for spoken reps (e.g. "bench press 5 reps"), parses and POSTs to backend, then renders a scrollable history from GET /sets. No auth, no charts. Depends on backend subtask being deployed first.
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- Manual priority 1 inherited from parent epic (note_0fc06f66); scope and priority consistent with sibling backend subtask grooming
- Well-scoped frontend deliverable: static PWA with Web Speech API for spoken rep parsing, POST to backend, scrollable history via GET /sets
- Acceptance criteria already written and precise — installable PWA, microphone prompt, parse and save spoken set, history updates
- Blocked by backend subtask note_1529c486 deploying first; that task is already classified at priority 1 so sequencing is clear
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: PWA loads on mobile Chrome at fourfoldmedia.uk/apps/gym-tracker, microphone prompt appears, a spoken set is correctly parsed and saved, and the history view updates to show the new entry. App is installable as a PWA.

active_tasks.title: Research token usage data available from Anthropic API responses
active_tasks.body: Jimbo uses claude-sonnet-4-6. Each API response includes input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens. This subtask is research only — output feeds the code subtasks.
active_tasks.ai_rationale: Researching Anthropic API token usage fields to enable cost tracking for Jimbo, which is prerequisite for active token-tracking epic and impacts financial visibility.
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: A written summary exists listing which fields in Anthropic API responses carry token counts, the per-token pricing for models in use, and how those map to per-dispatch cost.

active_tasks.title: Build token cost reporting query or endpoint for Jimbo
active_tasks.body: Report should cover: total cost per period, cost per dispatch, token breakdown (input vs output vs cache). Format: SQL view, /api/reports/token-costs endpoint, or dashboard panel.
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- Directly tied to the active Jimbo/Hermes project (top active priority in snapshot), specifically the token-tracking epic `track-how-many-tokens-used-for-pr-cost-associated-track--note_da25d25`
- Manual priority is already set to 1 by a human reviewer, confirming this-quarter importance; Jimbo velocity is actively being improved so cost visibility is operationally needed now
- This is an epic node with at least one child subtask already groomed (note_572bc315 — the REST endpoint); the epic serves as the umbrella covering SQL view, endpoint, and dashboard panel delivery options
- No duplicates detected; the parent epic and sibling tasks are complementary, not overlapping
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: A working query or endpoint returns aggregated token usage and estimated cost grouped by time period and optionally by note_id or dispatch_id, verified against real dispatch data with non-zero figures.

active_tasks.title: Instrument Jimbo pipeline to capture token usage per dispatch
active_tasks.body: Hook into Anthropic SDK calls in Jimbo/Hermes. Store: dispatch_id, note_id, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens, estimated_cost_usd, recorded_at.
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- Directly serves the active Jimbo/Hermes project (top active project in snapshot) and sits under the explicit token-tracking epic `track-how-many-tokens-used-for-pr-cost-associated-track--note_da25d25`, confirming this-quarter importance
- manual_priority is already 1 and acceptance criteria are fully written — token metrics persisted per dispatch and queryable; no ambiguity about done
- As an epic node itself, this orchestrates the SDK hooking work that sibling subtasks (research note_c01d15cd, reporting note_d1e09925, endpoint note_572bc315) depend on for real data to flow
- No duplicates detected; sibling tasks are complementary — this is the data-capture layer, others handle reporting
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: After each dispatch run, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache tokens, estimated_cost_usd, and model name are persisted. A query for any completed dispatch returns its token metrics.

active_tasks.title: Create DB schema for token usage metrics per dispatch
active_tasks.body: Design and apply the database migration for storing token usage records.
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- Directly supports the active Jimbo/Hermes project (top active priority in snapshot) and sits under the explicit token-tracking parent epic `note_6bfd014b` (Instrument Jimbo pipeline), which itself is under the top-level epic for AI cost tracking
- Acceptance criteria are fully specified and unambiguous: migration must create `token_usage` table with all named columns and run without errors — scope is locked and completable in a single sitting
- Manual priority is already 1 and prior AI analysis agreed; no reason to diverge — the schema is the foundational dependency before any token data can be captured or reported
- No duplicates detected; sibling tasks (research, instrumentation, reporting endpoint) are complementary and this DB migration is the prerequisite layer for all of them
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: A migration exists that creates a token_usage table with columns: id, dispatch_id, note_id, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens, estimated_cost_usd, recorded_at. Migration runs without errors.

active_tasks.title: Build Hono API routes and SQLite schema for voice gym tracker
active_tasks.body: Backend piece of the voice gym tracker. Two routes: POST /sets (accept exercise, reps, sets fields), GET /sets (return recent history). One SQLite table: gym_sets (id, timestamp, exercise, reps, sets). Deploy on jimbo-api with Hono + Caddy on existing infra.
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- Manual priority 1 inherited from parent epic (note_0fc06f66 — Voice gym tracker PWA), confirmed by the groomer; no reason to diverge
- Well-scoped backend subtask: two explicit routes (POST /sets, GET /sets), one named SQLite table, and a specific deployment target (jimbo-api with Hono + Caddy)
- Acceptance criteria already written and precise — prerequisites are clear, output is testable, and the task is executable in a single sitting
- Acts as the prerequisite backend before any frontend PWA work can proceed, giving it clear dependency value
active_tasks.acceptance_criteria: POST /api/gym/sets accepts JSON with exercise/reps/sets and persists to SQLite. GET /api/gym/sets returns the last 50 entries as JSON. Both routes return correct HTTP status codes. Migration runs without errors.

active_tasks.title: localshout-next #193: Hide bottom nav when keyboard is active
active_tasks.body:
GitHub issue: localshout-next#193

On mobile, the bottom navigation tab bar persists when keyboard is open, eating ~60px of viewport space. Use visualViewport API to detect keyboard presence.

Repo: ~/development/localshout-next
active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- LocalShout is the top active project targeting App Store/TestFlight submission; mobile UX polish is on the critical path and this is a known standard defect
- The fix is well-scoped: use the visualViewport API to detect keyboard presence and hide the bottom nav (~60px reclaimed viewport space) — completable in a single sitting
- Manual priority is already 1, confirming this-quarter importance; no reason to diverge
- No duplicates spotted in active tasks; this is a specific GitHub issue (localshout-next#193) with a clear implementation path

active_tasks.title: Get your name out there
active_tasks.body: Get your name out there

active_tasks.ai_rationale:

active_tasks.ai_rationale:
- Aligns with the active Jimbo/Hermes project (top active project in snapshot) — a GitHub issue maker would make Jimbo directly useful during development work, fitting the "Build & Ship Products" goal and the push to make Jimbo genuinely useful day-to-day
- Priority 1 (this quarter) is appropriate because Jimbo velocity is an active concern and developer tooling integrations like GitHub issue creation are concrete, shippable features
- Body is near-identical to the title (just an abbreviation "Gh issue maker") — no scoping details, AC, or implementation notes present, which limits actionability confidence
- No direct duplicates spotted in active tasks; the note is distinct from other Jimbo instrumentation/reporting tasks currently in flight
active_tasks.suggested_ac: Jimbo can create a new GitHub issue in a specified repository. User provides issue title and body. Jimbo confirms issue creation with a link.
